name: Pluralsight Flow Engineering Analytics API
what_it_does: Engineering analytics platform (formerly GitPrime, acquired by Pluralsight) with REST API — enabling engineering leaders to track DORA metrics, measure developer productivity via Git activity analysis, analyze code churn, review cycle times, and identify delivery risks across GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, and Azure DevOps repositories.
use_cases: Tracking DORA metrics via Pluralsight Flow API for engineering performance measurement
Analyzing developer contributions via Flow API for team productivity insights
Measuring code review cycle time via Flow API for delivery efficiency improvement
Monitoring code churn via Flow API for technical debt and rework identification
Exporting engineering metrics via Flow API for leadership dashboards and OKR tracking
not_for: Individual developer performance reviews without ethical framework (misuse risk)
Teams on systems not integrating with GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket/Azure DevOps
Real-time code review or deployment monitoring
best_when: An enterprise engineering leader needs comprehensive Git analytics and DORA metrics with Pluralsight's broader learning platform — particularly teams already using Pluralsight skills.
avoid_when: You want developer-positive metrics culture (Flow has been criticized for individual surveillance), or are not on Pluralsight platform.

auth.notes: API token via Authorization: Token {api_key} header (non-standard format). API key from Flow account settings. No OAuth2. REST API at flow.pluralsight.com/api/v2/. Read-only analytics API. No webhooks. GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket/Azure DevOps integration required for data.

pricing.paid_tiers: Part of Pluralsight Skills+Flow bundle; estimated $30-50/user/month
pricing.notes: US company (Draper, UT). Pluralsight (NASDAQ: PS) acquired GitPrime 2019 for $170M. GitPrime was founded by Travis Kimmel and Ben Thompson. Pluralsight went private (Francisco Partners) 2021. Flow: Git analytics, DORA metrics, code churn, review metrics, rework, new work, legacy refactor. Individual contributor metrics controversial — criticized for developer surveillance. Dashboard focus: engineering leader reports. Integration: GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps. Used by: large enterprises. Authorization: Token header (non-standard — not 'Bearer'). Bundled with Pluralsight Skills for learning + analytics.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Authorization: Token {key} (non-standard). Read-only analytics. GitHub/GitLab required. No webhooks. Individual metrics controversial. Pluralsight Skills bundle. US company. SOC2/GDPR.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: All read operations are idempotent — historical analytics data is deterministic. Team and contributor reads idempotent by ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Read-only API — analytics data only, no write operations
Individual contributor metrics: misuse risk for developer performance evaluation — use team-level only
GitHub/GitLab/Bitbucket/Azure DevOps integration required before any data available via API
Pluralsight went private (Francisco Partners 2021) — pricing and product direction may change
US data residency only — verify for EU compliance requirements

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Non-standard Token auth header. SOC2/GDPR certified. Individual developer data (commit patterns, PR activity) is sensitive. US data residency. Private equity ownership (Francisco Partners) — financial risk. Read-only analytics reduces breach impact. Integration credentials (GitHub OAuth) must be secured. Individual metrics raise privacy/labor law considerations.
